prompt:
Ticket from the release-tooling backlog, assigning to you:

Our environment provisioner runs steps like provision VM -> configure DNS -> announce to service registry, each step consuming the previous step's output. When step 3 dies (or an operator hits cancel in the UI) we currently just... stop, leaving half-provisioned junk behind that on-call has to delete by hand. We need a tiny orchestration core, pipeline.ts, exporting runPipeline(stages, input, opts?).

A stage is { name, run(input, signal), cleanup?() }. run may be sync or async; its return value feeds the next stage; the final stage's value resolves the pipeline. An empty stage list just resolves with the input.

Rollback rules (this is the whole point, get them exact):
- If a stage throws, every stage that COMPLETED before it gets its cleanup called, in reverse order, one at a time (each awaited). The failing stage did not complete — its cleanup must not run. Then reject with the stage's error.
- A cleanup that itself throws must not stop the remaining cleanups and must not replace the original error.
- opts.signal (AbortSignal) cancels: already-aborted means reject with its reason before running anything. Aborted mid-run means pass it through to the stage (stages get the signal as their second arg), wait for the in-flight stage to settle either way, skip all later stages, roll back whatever completed — and note a stage that completes successfully AFTER the abort landed still counts as completed and gets rolled back. Always reject with the abort reason, only after rollback finishes.

Acceptance tests: test_pipeline.ts (`node --test test_pipeline.ts`). They encode the ordering exactly; do not touch them. No libraries.
